channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: 'WarshGPT': How Wall Street is adapting to the Fed's new era of communication
item.description: Investment firms are readying for an era with less public commentary from the U.S. central bank. Some are relying on AI for an edge.

item.title: U.S. military says it has completed the latest round of strikes against Iran, amid more disruptions to shipping
item.description: U.S. Central Command says it continues to enforce a naval blockade against Iran, while Kuwait and Bahrain say they have intercepted more Iranian projectiles.

item.title: Korean beauty products are becoming mainstream in the U.S. Why there may be even more growth ahead
item.description: Morgan Stanley forecasts that K-beauty sales in the U.S. can reach approximately $4 billion in 2026.

item.title: How are prediction markets taxed? The IRS hasn’t provided guidance yet
item.description: Experts say the lack of federal guidance makes it's unclear on how prediction markets winnings should be reported and levied.

item.title: These are the most oversold stocks after a rough week on Wall Street
item.description: The major averages posted weekly losses, but a few tech stocks — such as Oracle and IBM — sold off especially hard.

item.title: Trump rips Canada as wildfire smoke spreads, says U.S. will add pollution cost to tariffs
item.description: The smoke coming from active wildfires in Canada has led to unhealthy air quality in swaths of the U.S., which could affect the World Cup final in New Jersey.

item.title: The White House is dictating access to frontier AI models, shifting power from tech giants, sources say
item.description: The Trump administration is taking steps to control who gets access to the latest frontier models, people familiar with the matter told CNBC.

item.title: Inside the Chinese fraud rings stealing billions from banks and retailers
item.description: Chinese organized crime groups are making as much as $1 billion annually in tap-to-pay fraud schemes targeting retailers and banks.

item.title: Taco Bell says it has removed lettuce linked to cyclosporiasis outbreak from its restaurants
item.description: Taco Bell, which the CDC linked to the cyclosporiasis outbreak, will likely recover soon from the health safety scare, according to analysts.

item.title: Economic outlook is worsening and Trump is getting blamed, CNBC survey finds
item.description: The public is as depressed about the economy as it has been since the years just after the pandemic, according to the All-America Economic Survey.

item.title: Democratic socialists top MAGA candidates among voters in CNBC's All-America poll
item.description: The survey's findings come as democratic socialist candidates win Democratic primaries following the 2025 election of New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani.

item.title: California's billionaire tax: What's at risk for the biggest state economy in America
item.description: California's billionaire tax would generate quick revenue, but Norway's example shows the longer-term economic impact of targeting the wealthy is unclear.

item.title: Leather jacket worn by Nvidia CEO goes for just under $1 million at Sotheby's auction
item.description: The high price for the garment is a sign that collectors are looking to bid on artifacts and collectibles from the artificial intelligence boom.

item.title: Tesla's charts point to a decline ahead, says Carter Worth
item.description: The technical analyst breaks down where he sees Tesla shares going.

item.title: S&P 500 closes lower, Nasdaq falls more than 1% as chip stocks suffer: Live updates
item.description: The moves come after a decline in chipmakers dragged the broader market lower and the U.S. continued to strike Iran overnight.

item.title: Chinese AI has leveled up, and brought renewed focus on the open weight model shift
item.description: It's the latest AI model from China to close the performance gap with leading U.S. AI labs.

item.title: Epstein victims blast Trump attorney general nominee Todd Blanche after meeting
item.description: "Todd Blanche treated the meeting as a mere 'check-the-box' exercise intended to secure votes for his confirmation," said Epstein survivor Dani Bensky.

item.title: Fat cat? Weight-loss drugs may be coming for your overweight pet
item.description: Startups are testing GLP-1 treatments for overweight cats while pet-food giants are leaning into longevity products as owners spend more on animal health.

item.title: Ukrainian drone attacks kill seven warehouse workers in Russia, spark fire at Moscow region oil depot
item.description: Russian governors say Ukrainian drones struck two online retail warehouses. Ukrainian President Zelenskyy says Kyiv hit military logistics facilities.

item.title: FAA lets Boeing sign off on 737 Max, 787 airworthiness certificates again
item.description: The move is a vote of confidence in Boeing from the U.S. government.

item.title: Meta's latest move in the AI talent war — plus Cramer's 4 quick hits on the market
item.description: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

item.title: There are only days left to get $1,200 from Raisin for opening a high-yield savings account or CD
item.description: Savings marketplace Raisin is offering new customers up to $1,200 for opening a high-yield savings account or CD.

item.title: Iraq and Syria sign agreement to restore oil pipeline that would provide alternative to Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi is visiting the U.S. this week. He met with President Donald Trump at the White House on Tuesday.

item.title: SpaceX stock has cratered nearly 23% since the company joined the Nasdaq-100
item.description: SpaceX's stock plunged further on Friday, a day after it aborted a test flight for its Starship rocket at the last second.

item.title: Anthropic in early talks with Meta to acquire compute power
item.description: The talks come weeks after Anthropic announced a similar deal with Elon Musk's SpaceX to use the computing capacity at its Colossus 1 data center

item.title: Retail and Wall Street are underwater on SpaceX — but not going down without a fight
item.description: Good times for SpaceX are proving short-lived as Elon Musk’s intergalactic IPO is now giving fodder to bears.

item.title: Amazon's Zoox issues software recall after robotaxi drove into heavy smoke
item.description: Last month, an unoccupied Zoox robotaxi drove into an active emergency fire scene that was clouded with smoke, the company said.

item.title: Apple, Nvidia vie for title of world's most valuable company
item.description: Nvidia shares have underperformed in 2026 as Wall Street shifts to companies powering the infrastructure AI buildout.

item.title: Netflix stock falls as earnings forecast disappoints, company says it will give fewer engagement updates
item.description: The streaming giant said it would cut back on the frequency of its "What We Watched" reports, which provide a picture of engagement.

item.title: How big is the great wealth transfer? It could be over $100 trillion or $36 trillion
item.description: Two studies have raised new questions about the size and impact of the coming great wealth transfer.
